title: Carnage on the Estoril Coast
title_candidates: Quinta da Marinha Eats the Field
theme: A bloodbath at Quinta da Marinha: Williams' double-digit lead shrinks to five as the entire field takes turns posting eights, nines, tens and an eleven
opening_hook: Open with the scoreboard arithmetic — a Trophy lead of 11 sliced to 5, a Green Jacket lead changing hands, a Wooden Spoon gap blown out to 22 — and pin it to the fact that Quinta da Marinha turned six grown men into demolition contractors
foreshadow: Williams arrives at the turn with a comfortable Trophy cushion — set up the 8-11 stretch where 14 shots vanish
Mullin starts the day as Green Jacket leader — flag the 12th where the lead slips
Neumann opens with a 10 at the 1st — foreshadow the front-nine haemorrhage that buries his Spoon hopes for good

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Williams still leads but the 11-shot cushion he carried into the round is down to five after a back-nine collapse (8-11) that bled 14 shots; Jon Baker closes hard and the race is suddenly live going into the final round

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Lead change: Mullin began the day six clear on Gross but posted his worst round to date (26 pts) with a quad at the 12th; Baker drew level at the 12th, Mullin briefly drew level back at the 15th, and Baker emerged two ahead by the close

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Neumann was eight adrift overnight; an opening 10, a 9 at the 4th, an 8 at the 9th and an 11 at the 10th blew the gap out to 22. Barring something miraculous in R4, the Spoon is engraved

players.arc: Holds the Trophy lead but bleeds it from 11 to 5 — a triple-bogey/double-bogey/quad/quintuple stretch from the 8th to the 11th (14 shots gone) plus another quad at the 13th turns a procession into a contest
The quiet beneficiary — no headline disasters, draws level for the Green Jacket lead at the 12th and ends the day two clear of Mullin and five behind Williams with everything to play for
Started the round as Green Jacket leader; posts his worst TEG round to date (26 pts), with a quad at the 12th the moment the jacket changes hands — drew level briefly at the 15th but couldn't hold on
A grim middle stretch — 9 shots dropped over holes 3-5, a 10 at the 8th and a quad at the 12th — eventually stops the bleeding with a birdie at the 17th, the only oasis in a desert
Quietly endures, but bookends his card with quads at the 13th and 18th; stays well out of the Trophy and Jacket conversations
A round of near-total collapse: 17 shots bled over the opening five holes (10 at the 1st, 9 at the 4th), then an 8 at the 9th and an 11 at the 10th — the Wooden Spoon gap balloons from 8 to 22

venue_notes: TEG's 2nd visit to Quinta da Marinha (Robert Trent Jones Sr., parkland, elevated targets and abundant bunkering above the Estoril coast) — the elevated greens and bunkering did precisely what they're designed to do; second visit to the Lisbon Coast for TEG
